title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Opens in Berlin: Systems Biology Replaces Single-Target Approach
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity convened April 8-9 in Berlin, marking a conceptual shift in aging science. Researchers are moving from searching for a single anti-aging molecule toward understanding aging as a progressive loss of coordination between mitochondria, microbiota, metabolism, and immunity -- with biological resilience as the new north star.

title: Fatty Acids Identified as Natural Senolytics: Ferroptosis Opens New Therapeutic Direction
description: University of Minnesota researchers published in Cell Press Blue that specific polyunsaturated fatty acids selectively eliminate senescent cells by triggering ferroptosis -- a distinct mechanism from all existing senolytics. Senescent cells' uniquely high iron levels and oxidative stress make them vulnerable to this lipid-driven cell death pathway, suggesting a natural therapeutic avenue for IPF, osteoarthritis, and age-related frailty.

title: UTMB Moody Longevity Trial Launches: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin Tested Together
description: The University of Texas Medical Branch launched the Moody Longevity Trial, one of the largest human tests of geroprotective drug combinations. The trial targets adults over 55 and tests whether rapamycin, semaglutide (GLP-1), and dapagliflozin (SGLT2) can measurably slow biological aging pace as tracked by epigenetic clocks and functional biomarkers.

title: David Sinclair Assembles Team to Combat NIH Funding Cuts to Aging Research
description: David Sinclair posted on X that longevity research is being defunded just as the U.S. needs to compete globally, and that he has assembled a team with a launch planned for April 2026. The action follows federal budget cuts affecting the NIA and aging research programs at Harvard, raising alarm in the scientific community about America's longevity research competitiveness.

title: GLP-1 Drugs Emerge as First True Longevity Therapeutics: Critical Mass of Evidence
description: A Nature Biotechnology review and multiple 2025-2026 analyses now position GLP-1 agonists as the closest existing drug class to a true geroprotective: reducing all-cause mortality, systemic inflammation, cancer risk, and cardiovascular events while improving metabolic flexibility. Researchers argue GLP-1s may target more hallmarks of aging simultaneously than any other approved drug, with microdosing protocols now being explored specifically for healthy aging.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Longevity Debate Shifts to Systems Biology
description: International congress in Berlin brings together experts in mitochondria, microbiota, senescence, and systems medicine to develop a coordinated framework for understanding aging beyond isolated pathways.

title: Mitochondrial Dysfunction Is the Mother of All Hallmarks of Aging
description: Volkmar Weissig presenting at Targeting Longevity 2026 challenges single-pathway approaches to aging, arguing mitochondrial dysfunction underlies virtually all aging hallmarks and must be addressed systemically rather than in isolation.

title: Fatty Acids as Senolytics: Ferroptosis Kills Senescent Cells via Lipid Mechanism
description: University of Minnesota researchers identified polyunsaturated lipids that selectively eliminate senescent cells via ferroptosis, exploiting their high iron and oxidative stress load. Published in Cell Press Blue, this is the first demonstration of lipids functioning as senolytics via this distinct death mechanism.

title: Fight Aging! Newsletter April 8, 2026: Axon Regeneration via AhR Signaling
description: Nature paper describes how AhR pathway activation enables axon regeneration after injury in the optic nerve, with potential implications for reversing age-related neurodegeneration and vision loss.

title: Oral Microbiome Dysbiosis Linked to Cognitive Decline and Dementia Risk
description: New research in eBioMedicine establishes a connection between oral microbiome composition and cognitive aging trajectories, suggesting that dental health and oral flora may be underappreciated intervention targets for dementia prevention.

title: NR0B2 Identified as Key Regulator of Osteoarthritis Progression
description: Researchers publishing in Nature Communications identified NR0B2 as a regulator of cartilage homeostasis and osteoarthritis progression, opening a new molecular target for age-related joint disease therapy.

title: UTMB Moody Longevity Trial Tests Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin in Adults Over 55
description: University of Texas Medical Branch launched the Moody Longevity Trial, testing three geroprotective drugs -- rapamycin (mTOR), semaglutide (GLP-1), and dapagliflozin (SGLT2) -- to measure biological aging slowdown in adults over 55.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Rapamycin Dosing Trial in 84 Older Adults
description: NIA-funded, placebo-controlled trial will determine evidence-based dosing for rapamycin's anti-aging use in approximately 84 adults aged 65-90, focusing on precision dosing to achieve biological effects without excess immunosuppression.

title: David Sinclair Assembles Team to Counter Longevity Research Funding Cuts
description: Sinclair warns on X that longevity research is being defunded at a critical moment of global competitiveness, and announces an assembled team with an April 2026 launch to fill the gap left by federal budget cuts to the NIA.

title: GLP-1 Microdosing for Longevity: Science Behind the Off-Label Trend
description: Researchers are exploring GLP-1 agonists at sub-therapeutic doses specifically for anti-aging purposes, driven by mounting evidence of systemic effects on inflammation, cardiovascular health, cancer risk reduction, and potential slowing of cellular aging.

title: Midlife Behavioral Patterns Predict Lifespan in Stanford Killifish Study
description: Published in Science, this Stanford study tracked killifish throughout their lives and found that movement and sleep patterns in midlife reliably predict lifespan. Aging does not unfold gradually but in sudden stepwise transitions, with implications for early human intervention via wearables.

title: SGLT2 Inhibitors Lengthen Telomeres and Reduce Cellular Senescence in Humans
description: A 2025 Cell Reports Medicine study showed 6 months of SGLT2 inhibitor treatment (henagliflozin) produced measurable telomere elongation in humans -- the first FDA-approved drug class to demonstrate this effect. A companion review confirmed SGLT2 inhibitors reduce SASP markers, activate AMPK and SIRT1, and modulate multiple hallmarks of aging beyond glucose control.

title: PEARL Trial: Weekly 10mg Rapamycin Improves Lean Mass and Pain in Women Over 48 Weeks
description: The PEARL trial -- a 48-week double-blind RCT -- found that once-weekly 10mg rapamycin significantly improved lean tissue mass and self-reported pain in women, with no increase in serious adverse events versus placebo. A PhenoAge modeling analysis estimated biological age reduction of nearly 4 years in rapamycin-treated participants.

title: Longevity Graduates to Central Therapeutic Category at JPMorgan Healthcare 2026
description: At JPM2026, the consensus shifted from debating whether aging biology can produce medicines to defining how to execute at scale. GLP-1 success is being used as proof that modifying metabolic health can alter the aging trajectory itself, with women's health and sex-specific biology gaining major traction.

title: Life Biosciences FDA IND Cleared: First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Underway
description: Life Biosciences received FDA IND clearance for ER-100, an AAV gene therapy using OSK reprogramming factors to reverse vision loss from glaucoma and NAION. Phase 1 dosing in humans began in Q1 2026, marking the world's first partial cellular reprogramming trial.

title: BCL-2 Inhibition Selectively Targets Senescent Fibroblasts in Pulmonary Fibrosis
description: New research demonstrates that BCL-2 inhibition can selectively clear senescent fibroblasts in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, opening a senolytic therapeutic avenue for a disease with few effective options.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences RLS-1496 Phase 1 Positive Results: Senolytic for Skin Aging
description: Rubedo Life Sciences announced positive preliminary Phase 1 results for RLS-1496 in patients with plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging, validating the senolytic approach for dermatological applications and paving the way for expanded indications.

title: Bryan Johnson Launches $1M/Year Immortals Program with 1,500 Waitlist Signups
description: Johnson's Immortals program offers a $1 million annual concierge longevity protocol combining AI-guided personalization, 24/7 biomarker monitoring, and cutting-edge therapies, attracting over 1,500 expressions of interest within 30 hours of launch.

title: Lifespan Heritability Revised to 55%: Genes Matter More Than Previously Thought
description: A Weizmann Institute study published in Science revised human lifespan heritability to approximately 55% when extrinsic mortality (accidents, infections) is excluded -- more than double previous estimates of 20-25%. This brings lifespan heritability in line with most other biological traits and reinvigorates the case for studying longevity genetics.

title: Longevity Biotech Q1 2026: $3.74B Raised, Full-Year Projection at $8-9B
description: DLT intelligence platform data shows Q1 2026 saw 49 longevity biotech financing events totaling $3.74B -- a 56% increase over Q1 2025. The median deal size of $21.8M signals a maturing pipeline graduating from early-stage to clinical-stage companies.

title: Physical Activity Associated with Significant All-Cause Mortality Reduction
description: A major PLOS Medicine study quantifies the dose-response relationship between physical activity and all-cause mortality reduction, reinforcing exercise as the single most potent geroprotective intervention available -- ahead of any existing drug.

title: Severe Infections Accelerate Dementia Risk: New Population-Level Evidence
description: Large population study in PLOS Medicine finds that severe systemic infections significantly accelerate dementia onset, pointing to infection-driven neuroinflammation as a modifiable risk factor for cognitive aging.

title: AI Is Accelerating Longevity Drug Discovery: Industry Update 2026
description: By 2026, AI platforms like Insilico Medicine's are designing drugs from target identification to Phase 2 trial in record time. A December 2025 AI analysis mapped a century of aging research and identified previously unknown connections between microbiome engineering and senescent cell clearance -- a direction no human researcher had prioritized.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: NK Cell Rejuvenation and Mitochondrial Senolytics
description: February highlights include: mitochondrial stress shown to enhance senolytic efficacy (Nature Aging), restoration of natural killer cell function in older adults (Aging Cell), and laromestrocel stem cell therapy showing positive Phase 2b results for aging frailty.

title: Exerkines and Extracellular Vesicles: Exercise Benefits May Be Transferable as Therapy
source: Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology / Fight Aging!
description: Research in Frontiers explores how muscle-derived exerkines carried in extracellular vesicles mediate the systemic benefits of exercise, raising the prospect that exercise's anti-aging effects could eventually be captured in a therapeutic formulation for those unable to exercise.

title: Longevity Biotech Funding Tracker: Function Health $298M Series B Leads 12-Deal Period
description: Twelve pure-play longevity companies raised over $530M between November 2025 and January 2026. Function Health's $298M Series B at a $2.5B valuation dominated, alongside mTOR-targeting Aeovian ($55M Series B) and cellular reprogramming startup Junevity ($10M seed extension).
